title: 10 best herbal essential oils for plantar fasciitis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for plantar fasciitis are natural extracts from plants that help alleviate the pain and inflammation associated with this condition. They work by reducing swelling, relieving pressure on the affected area, and promoting healing. The best herbal essential oils for plantar fasciitis include Eucalyptus globulus, Cymbopogon citratus, Zingiber officinale, Melaleuca alternifolia, Curcuma longa, Rosmarinus officinalis, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Cinnamomum camphora, Salvia officinalis, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, plantar fasciitis can cause chronic pain, limit mobility, and disrupt daily activities, leading to a reduced quality of life and increased stress levels.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its cooling and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, it helps to reduce pain and swelling in the affected area, making it easier to move around comfortably. The menthol present in peppermint oil also helps to relax tense muscles and improves blood circulation, which can aid in reducing stiffness and discomfort in the feet and ankles. Regular use of peppermint essential oils may help alleviate chronic plantar fasciitis pain, promoting a faster recovery and improved mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, help reduce pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis.
Analgesic properties: The analgesic compounds in peppermint oil, including menthol and camphor, provide relief from pain and discomfort in the affected area.
Cooling and warming properties: Peppermint oil's cooling and warming sensations help increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in menthol, a natural analgesic that helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Leaves: The leaves contain high concentrations of menthone, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and swelling in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh mentha x piperita leaves at dawn in the summer season weighing approximately 2 kilograms.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold running water and air dry them for 3 hours.
Steam distill the dried mentha x piperita leaves using a still with a ratio of 1:4 leaf to water mixture for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a well-sealed container at a rate of approximately 30 milliliters per kilogram of leaves.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth within 24 hours of distillation to remove any impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce pain and swelling in the affected area. The oil's natural analgesic and antispasmodic compounds also help to relax tense muscles and improve circulation, allowing for faster healing and reduced stiffness. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oil has been shown to have a direct effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate pain signals sent to the brain and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been shown to reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of this essential oil can help protect the affected area from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing scar tissue formation.

Pain-relieving property: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been traditionally used to relieve pain and discomfort, making it a potential natural remedy for plantar fasciitis-related pain in the heel and arch of the foot.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian blue gum are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat musculoskeletal conditions, including plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in early morning when oil content is highest and dry them thoroughly within three hours.
Crush the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 100 grams of material.
Steep the powdered eucalyptus globulus in 500 milliliters of high-proof ethanol for four hours at room temperature, ensuring minimal oxidation and contamination.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining approximately 200 milliliters of liquid extract.

Distill the liquid extract using steam distillation or vacuum distillation, collecting approximately 10-15 milliliters of eucalyptus globulus essential oil within two hours, with a typical yield of 2-3%.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the affected area. The oil's methyl salicylate content provides a gentle yet effective way to soothe irritated tissues and relax tense muscles, helping to restore flexibility and mobility. Additionally, wintergreen oil's cooling sensation can help numb the pain, providing immediate relief for plantar fasciitis sufferers. Regular use of wintergreen essential oils can lead to long-term relief from chronic foot pain.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Wintergreen oil contains high levels of menthol, which has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve pain and reduce swelling in the feet, making it effective for treating plantar fasciitis.

Pain relief: The menthol and other compounds in wintergreen oil have a topical anesthetic effect, numbing the affected area and providing fast-acting pain relief from plantar fascia inflammation and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory: Wintergreen oil has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can help alleviate the chronic pain and stiffness associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wintergreen plant contain a high concentration of methyl salicylate, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Rhyzomes: Wintergreen rhyzomes are rich in salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, making them effective for reducing pain and discomfort in the heels and arches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems, approximately 50 grams, on a sunny morning in early summer.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 12 hours or until it reaches 10% moisture content.
Steam distill the dried material using a rotary evaporator with a ratio of 1:4 (material to water) and a temperature of 100°c, collecting the essential oil in a clean glass container.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper or a small piece of cloth to remove any impurities, then store it in an amber glass bottle.
Monitor the essential oil's color, consistency, and aroma over time, discarding it if it changes significantly or develops off-odors after 6 months of storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The oil's natural analgesic effects also ease discomfort and stiffness in the heel and arch, allowing for increased mobility and reduced pressure on the plantar fascia. Additionally, elder essential oil's antimicrobial properties promote a healthy environment, reducing the risk of infection and promoting faster healing. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief from chronic pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elder oil's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain associated with plantar fasciitis, allowing for easier movement and reduced discomfort.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of elder oil can help numb the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort caused by plantar fasciitis, making it easier to bear weight on the foot.
Antioxidant: Elder oil's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to plantar fasciitis and other chronic conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, elder roots are believed to provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them suitable for treating plantar fasciitis pain.
Rhyzomes: As a storage organ, rhyzomes contain concentrated amounts of medicinal compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers in quantities of 100-200g.
Steam distill the sambucus nigra flowers using 500ml of water for 60-90 minutes.
Combine 20ml of the resulting essential oil with 10ml of jojoba oil and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Apply topically to affected area 2-3 times daily, gently massaging into skin for 5-7 minutes.
Store the blended oil in a cool place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The oil's antimicrobial properties also prevent infection and promote healing, which can accelerate recovery from plantar fasciitis. Additionally, tea tree oil's analgesic and antispasmodic properties provide relief from discomfort and stiffness, allowing for a more comfortable gait and improved mobility.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: TTO's antibacterial properties can help prevent infection and promote healing by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that can exacerbate plantar fasciitis.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: TTO's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cineole, can help reduce swelling and pain in the heel and arch area, providing relief from Plantar Fasciitis symptoms.
Antioxidant Properties: TTO's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, which can worsen inflammation and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the tea tree plant contain high amounts of melaleuca oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Barks: Tea tree bark is rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which are known to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate the pain and discomfort caused by plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a trusted source in the morning for optimal potency and aroma.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris before processing.
Steam distill 100 grams of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves using 500 milliliters of distilled water at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to extract the essential oil.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper or cotton cloth to remove impurities and sediment within 15 minutes.
Store the final melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature, away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena flowers at dawn for optimal fragrance and therapeutic properties.
Dry the harvested flowers in small batches using air circulation for 7-10 days or until crisp.
Steam distill dried flowers using a clevenger apparatus for 2 hours to extract essential oil.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from water using a separating funnel at room temperature.
Store the rosa damascena essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at a consistent refrigerator temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties can provide relief from pain and swelling in the affected area. The oil's calming effects also help to reduce tension and stiffness in the foot, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties can help to relax the muscles and tendons surrounding the plantar fascia, reducing strain and discomfort associated with this common condition.

Dry the harvested lavender flowers using a food dehydrator or air circulation method within 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried lavender flowers in a still equipped with a condenser coil at a temperature of 100°c for 30 minutes.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid within an hour of distillation.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can reduce pain and discomfort in the heel and bottom of the foot. The oil's ability to relax muscles and improve circulation also helps to alleviate tension and strain on the plantar fascia, a band of tissue that runs along the bottom of the foot. Additionally, marjoram's natural antioxidant properties may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it a valuable addition to a treatment plan for plantar fasciitis sufferers.

Seeds: Contain a significant amount of calcium, magnesium, and zinc, which are essential for maintaining healthy bones and joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh origanum majorana leaves and dry them for 48 hours in a well-ventilated area.
Steam distill the dried herbs using 200ml of water at a temperature of 100°c for 60 minutes.
Collect the essential oil produced during distillation and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place for at least 24 hours before use.
Mix 10 drops of the origanum majorana essential oil with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling in the heel and arch area. The citrus scent of lemongrass oil also has a natural analgesic effect, providing instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, lemongrass oil's antispasmodic properties can help relax tense muscles and joints, promoting flexibility and range of motion. By applying lemongrass essential oil topically to the affected area, individuals with plantar fasciitis can experience significant reduction in pain and discomfort, allowing them to resume their daily activities with ease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like citral and geraniol, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, alleviating symptoms of plantar fasciitis.
Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic properties of lemongrass essential oil can help to numb the area, providing quick relief from pain and discomfort caused by plantar fasciitis.

Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass essential oil has antimicrobial compounds that can help prevent bacterial infections, which are common in areas with poor circulation or trauma, such as those affected by plantar fasciitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems contain high amounts of citral, a natural pain-relieving compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with plantar fasciitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning after dew has dried, collecting about 20 ounces for processing.
Clean the harvested leaves by gently rinsing them with distilled water and patting dry with a paper towel to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill the clean leaves in a stainless steel still using 10 gallons of distilled water at 212°f for 4 hours, collecting the essential oil as it separates.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, discarding any sediment that forms.
Store the filtered essential oil in a cool, dark place and use within 6 months, applying topically to affected areas 3-4 times daily for optimal relief from plantar fasciitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce pain and swelling in the affected area. The curcuminoid compound present in turmeric oil has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, leading to a decrease in inflammation. Additionally, turmeric's analgesic properties help to alleviate discomfort and numbness in the heel and arch of the foot, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness. This natural remedy can be applied topically or added to massages to provide relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling, pain, and stiffness associated with plantar fasciitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of turmeric oil help alleviate pain and discomfort in the heel and ankle area, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing plantar fasciitis symptoms.
Antioxidant: Turmeric oil's antioxidant property helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, promoting healing and tissue repair in the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, the primary active compound responsible for anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of volatile oils and curcuminoids, making it an ideal part for extracting medicinal essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric rhizomes weighing about 500 grams from local farmers or your garden.
Clean and chop the harvested turmeric into small pieces of approximately 1-2 centimeters.
Dry the chopped turmeric pieces in a clean, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam-distill the dried turmeric pieces using a steam distillation apparatus with a yield of about 5%.
Filter and bottle the obtained curcuma longa essential oil, store it in a dark glass container at room temperature.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat plantar fasciitis with herbal essential oils is by using a combination of warm compresses infused with peppermint, lavender, and wintergreen oils. Apply the mixture directly to the affected area for 10-15 minutes, three times a day. This helps reduce inflammation, relaxes tense muscles, and soothes pain. Additionally, massage a blend of ginger, frankincense, and cypress oils onto the foot to promote circulation and relieve stiffness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to plantar fasciitis that are treated with herbal essential oils are conditions such as tendinitis, shin splints, and stress fractures. Eucalyptus oil is commonly used to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in these areas, while peppermint oil can help ease muscle tension and stiffness. Additionally, ginger oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in the healing process.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area. remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol (1,8-Cineole): This terpene helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with plantar fasciitis by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent.
Limonene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce pain and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from plantar fasciitis.
Cineophyllin (Phenolic Compound): This compound exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the plantar fascia, providing relief from plantar fasciitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make essential oils for plantar fasciitis due to their high content of eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Barks: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Stems: Used in some cases to make essential oils with anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties that can help alleviate plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1000g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves on a dry day with a moisture content of 10-20%.
Clean and dry the leaves for 2 hours to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill the leaves in a still using 1:1 water to leaf ratio at a temperature of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the water and transfer it to a clean glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh stalks of cymbopogon citratus at dawn when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested stalks in a warm shaded area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried stalks at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to separate essential oil from water.
Collect and filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the obtained essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 kilograms of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes from mature plants at peak potency during full moon.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm place with air circulation for 7-10 days to achieve 10-12% moisture content.
Steam distill 5 kilograms of dried curcuma longa rhizomes in a 10-liter still for 4-6 hours to collect essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation water through decantation and transfer it to a clean glass container.
Store the collected essential oil in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight in a tightly sealed glass container.

The oil's warming and analgesic effects also help relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, which in turn reduces pressure on the plantar fascia. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help prevent infections that can exacerbate the condition. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Cinnamomum zeylanicum essential oils can provide relief and alleviate symptoms associated with plantar fasciitis. Regular use may also aid in the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: Helps to reduce inflammation and pain associated with plantar fasciitis due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Linalool: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing swelling and promoting healing of the affected area in plantar fasciitis.
Cinnamyl acetate: Displays analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to produce an essential oil that helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, are used to produce an essential oil that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in treating plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinnamomum zeylanicum leaves and stems by hand in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a copper still for 2-3 hours to separate oil from water.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel and then filter it through a coffee filter.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum camphora essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and borneol, have been shown to reduce pain and swelling in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Cinnamomum camphora essential oils can help alleviate the discomfort and tension associated with plantar fasciitis, allowing for faster recovery and improved mobility. Regular use may also reduce the risk of chronic pain and future episodes.
remedy_constituents: Cadinene: Helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with plantar fasciitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Camphene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which contribute to its effectiveness in alleviating pain and discomfort in the plantar fascia.
Bornyl acetate: Displays anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area, thus providing relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties to help relieve pain and reduce inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Barks: Used for their camphor content, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to help reduce pain and prevent infection in the affected area.
Rhyzomes: Used for their camphor content, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to help relieve pain and reduce swelling in the plantar fascia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh cinnamomum camphora leaves and twigs for the next step.
Steam distill the harvested cinnamomum camphora leaves and twigs for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oils contain compounds like borneol and camphor that reduce inflammation and ease pain in the plantar fascia. It also improves circulation, which helps in reducing swelling and promoting healing in the affected area. Additionally, the oils' antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles, reducing tension and strain on the plantar fascia, thereby alleviating discomfort and pain associated with plantar fasciitis. This makes Salvia officinalis essential oils a potential natural remedy for plantar fasciitis.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Reduces inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis.
Rosmarinic acid: Exerts potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
Ursolic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing pain and discomfort caused by plantar fasciitis, while also promoting tissue repair and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The essential oil extracted from the leaves of Salvia officinalis is commonly used to treat plantar fasciitis due to its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

Flowers: The essential oil extracted from the flowers of Salvia officinalis is also used to treat plantar fasciitis due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce pain and inflammation.

Buds: The essential oil extracted from the buds of Salvia officinalis is used to treat plantar fasciitis due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature salvia officinalis leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Steam distill 200g of salvia officinalis leaves with 1 liter of water at a temperature of 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water through decantation for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities for 10 minutes.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature of 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with plantar fasciitis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's potent compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area. The oil's ability to relax muscles and promote blood flow also aids in soothing irritated tissues. Additionally, its natural decongestant properties help to reduce fluid buildup and ease pressure on the plantar fascia, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the affected area, which can contribute to the discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the plantar fascia.
Caryophyllene oxide: Shows anti-inflammatory activity, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected area, providing relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate plantar fasciitis pain.
Leaves: They contain various compounds with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can provide relief from plantar fasciitis pain.
Stems: They contain essential oils that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce plantar fasciitis pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom time when purple color is fully developed.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a single layer on paper bags or screens for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a distillation apparatus at a temperature range of 100 to 120 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separatory funnel within 24 hours.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles away from heat and light sources at room temperature.
